2605.10025.title: Medical Incident Causal Factors and Preventive Measures Generation Using Tag-based Example Selection in Few-shot Learning
2605.10025.paper_summary: In high-stakes domains such as healthcare, the reliability of Large Language Models (LLMs) is critical, particularly when generating clinical insights from incident reports. This study proposes a tag-based few-shot example selection method for prompting LLMs to generate background/causal factors and preventive measures from details of the medical incidents. For our experiments, we use the Japanese Medical Incident Dataset (JMID), a structured dataset of 3,884 real-world medical accident and near-miss reports. These reports are variably annotated with a wide range of tags--some include descriptive information (e.g., "medications," "blood transfusion therapy"). We compare three few-shot example selection strategies--random sampling, cosine similarity-based selection, and our proposed tag-based method--using GPT-4o and LLaMA 3.3. Results show that the tag-based approach achieves the highest precision and most stable generation behavior, while similarity-based selection often leads to unintended outputs and safety filter activation. These findings suggest that selecting examples based on human-interpretable dataset tags can improve generation precision and stability in clinical LLM applications.
2605.10025.paper_summary_zh: 在醫療等高風險領域，大型語言模型（LLMs）的可靠性至關重要，特別是在從事件報告中生成臨床洞察時。本研究提出了一種基於標籤的少量示例選擇方法，以促使LLMs從醫療事件的細節中生成背景/因果因素和預防措施。在我們的實驗中，我們使用日本醫療事件數據集（JMID），這是一個包含3,884個真實世界醫療事故和近乎事故報告的結構化數據集。這些報告的標註變化多端，涵蓋了各種標籤——有些包括描述性信息（例如，“藥物”，“輸血療法”）。我們比較了三種少量示例選擇策略——隨機抽樣、基於餘弦相似度的選擇，以及我們提出的基於標籤的方法——使用GPT-4o和LLaMA 3.3。結果顯示，基於標籤的方法達到了最高的精確度和最穩定的生成行為，而基於相似度的選擇則經常導致意外輸出和安全過濾器啟動。這些發現表明，根據人類可解釋的數據集標籤選擇示例可以提高臨床LLM應用中的生成精確度和穩定性。
2605.10025.author: Yuna Haseyama et.al.
2605.10025.authors: Yuna Haseyama, Tomoki Ito, Hiroki Sakaji, Itsuki Noda
